changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforced to k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enabled for consistency
Changed k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled to k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enforced for clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to information flow enforcement. However, minor naming adjustments were made for consistency and clarity. The use of TLS and mTLS is appropriate for securing data transfers in interconnected systems.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of 'enabled' or 'enforced' to clearly indicate the action being checked
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | information transfers on interconnected systems [
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(16) focuses on enforcing secure information flow between interconnected systems. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring secure network communication and data transfer. NetworkPolicies can enforce default deny rules to control ingress traffic, TLS can be enforced for secure data transmission, and service meshes like Istio can enable mutual TLS (mTLS) for secure service-to-service communication.
